description_long: This object is a long, double-edged sword with a straight, tapered blade that appears metallic and sharp. The blade is a dark gray, suggesting it is forged from steel or a similar metal, and it extends from a prominent, ornate crossguard. The crossguard is gold or brass in color, with angular, wing-like extensions that provide both protection and visual flair. The handle is black with gold accents, slightly contoured for grip, and ends in a pointed pommel, also gold-colored, which may serve as a counterweight. The sword's design is both functional and decorative, with a medieval or fantasy aesthetic suitable for ceremonial or combat use. No engravings or jewels are visible, but the color contrast and shape give it a distinguished appearance.
description: A long, double-edged sword with a dark metallic blade, gold crossguard, and black-and-gold contoured handle. The design is ornate yet functional, with a medieval or fantasy style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the blade, ornate gold crossguard, and the contoured handle with gold accents.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard appearing as a narrow line.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the blade, crossguard, and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the blade and handle, with minimal crossguard visibility.
attribute_handle_design: contoured black handle with gold accents and pointed pommel
